medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety/sleep
Triamcinolone 0.1% cream for dermatitis flares (short courses)
Omeprazole 20 mg as needed for reflux
health.mental_health: In recovery from an eating disorder and currently experiencing heightened anxiety and hypervigilance due to an emotionally abusive partner; working on a safety plan and coping skills with a therapist while preparing to leave.
recent_visits: Therapy session (weekly) focused on eating-disorder recovery and relationship safety planning
Dermatology follow-up for acne/eczema flare after stress-related skin picking
Primary care visit for sleep issues and anxiety screening
Dietitian check-in to maintain a consistent meal plan during high-stress weeks

finance.debt: About $18,000 in student loans; also carrying ~$2,400 on a credit card after covering most shared household groceries/utility gaps and paying for a new phone plan to separate accounts before leaving.
recent_transactions: Deposit for a room in a shared apartment in the Inner Sunset
New prepaid phone plan and SIM to keep communications private
Therapy copays and dietitian visit copay
Purchase of a locking document box for passport/birth certificate
One-way rideshare trips late evenings to avoid conflict at home

relationships: Partner: Lina K. (emotionally abusive; monitoring texts and pressuring him not to see friends)
Best friend: Jordan Reyes (trusted confidant; helping him plan a safe move-out)
Mentor teacher: Ms. Priya Desai (department lead; supportive but Marek keeps details limited)
Sibling: Tomas Banda (older brother in Sacramento; occasional check-ins and potential temporary backup housing)
Support group contact: Eli Nguyen (eating-disorder recovery peer from a local group)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low profile online; Instagram is private with limited followers, posts mostly classroom math memes and occasional Golden Gate Park photos. He has muted/blocked his partner from some stories and uses a secondary account to follow recovery resources.
private_conversations: Safety planning and exit timing from the relationship (where to stay, how to move belongings)
Discussing emotional abuse patterns and how to document incidents
Talking to his brother about a backup place to sleep if things escalate
Confiding in a friend about relapse fears related to eating-disorder recovery under stress

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 7:30am–4:30pm (arrival by 7:30, classes 8:15–3:10, office hours/meetings until 4:30); occasional evening grading
regular_appointments: Weekly therapy on Wednesdays at 6:00pm (telehealth from a private location)
Dietitian check-in every other Monday at 5:30pm
upcoming_events: Apartment key pickup and move-in for a shared room (planned for the 15th, early morning to avoid conflict)
District math professional development day next month
Coffee with Jordan to coordinate move logistics and safety check-in
schedule.daily_routine: Early commute by Muni, quick breakfast and packed lunch, teaches Algebra/Geometry, stays after school for tutoring, then grades and plans lessons at a café or library to avoid being at home; evenings include a short run or walk, skincare routine, and a structured dinner plan to support recovery.

professional.company: San Francisco Unified School District (public high school campus in the Richmond District)
projects: Building a differentiated Algebra 1 unit on linear functions with scaffolded practice sets
After-school tutoring roster for students at risk of failing
Co-writing common assessments with the math department
Classroom management plan refresh focused on restorative routines
professional.performance_issues: Occasional fatigue and distractedness after late-night conflicts at home; he has used a few last-minute sick days and sometimes completes grading at odd hours to maintain classroom readiness.
workplace_relationships: Works closely with Ms. Priya Desai (department lead) who has noticed stress and offered support
Friendly collaboration with Mr. Calvin Park (new teacher cohort) for lesson planning
Keeps boundaries with a colleague who gossips about staff personal lives, avoiding sharing details

hobbies: Running along the Embarcadero and in Golden Gate Park
Pickup basketball at the local rec center
Puzzle games and logic challenges (Sudoku, chess puzzles)
Exploring used bookstores and quiet cafés
Cooking simple meal-prep recipes to stay consistent in recovery
habits: Tracks meals using a recovery-focused plan (not calorie counting) to prevent relapse
Nightly skincare routine and avoids fragranced products due to dermatitis
Keeps a packed 'go bag' with essentials and documents in case he needs to leave quickly
Uses a separate email/phone number for housing and safety planning
Avoids alcohol and limits caffeine because it worsens anxiety and sleep
lifestyle.living_situation: Currently living with his partner in a small apartment; he is quietly arranging to move into a shared place with roommates and plans to leave when his partner is out to reduce risk of confrontation.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: No strict restrictions; focuses on regular balanced meals and snacks (protein + carbs + fats) to support eating-disorder recovery, with gentle structure and flexibility. Avoids very spicy foods when reflux flares.
